blocks.content: Monday. Something had shifted and Jonathan picked it up the moment he saw me.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: No cap you look like someone who finally exhaled. What happened after the movies?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh. I know your face. Something happened.

We'd walked back from the theater and the air was cold and she'd bumped into my shoulder and then didn't move away. And then we were holding still in a parking lot at 10pm saying nothing and saying everything. And then she looked at me the way that meant she wasn't going to do the thing unless I did. So I did. And it was real.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I am keeping my voice down. Relatively. My guy. Finally.
He was genuinely happy. Not smug — just happy the way someone is when they've been rooting for something and it finally worked out. I didn't say anything else about it. But I didn't stop him either.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan's reaction. What does it feel like to have him in your corner on this?

options.label: Good. He earned the right to be happy about it.
Larry keeps it close. It's still new. He doesn't want to jinx it.
